candidates.field_a: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics — Mpemba effect spectral theory
candidates.field_c: Neurodegenerative protein biochemistry — amyloid aggregation selectivity

key_papers.title: The Mpemba index and anomalous relaxation
key_papers.relevance: Defines Mpemba index and spectral mechanism — the core theory for the bridge

key_papers.title: Speedups in nonequilibrium thermal relaxation: Mpemba and related effects
key_papers.relevance: Comprehensive review through 2024; confirms ZERO biophysical Mpemba applications exist

key_papers.title: Amyloid assembly is dominated by misregistered kinetic traps on an unbiased energy landscape
key_papers.relevance: Amyloid MSM with spectral structure — the mathematical target for Mpemba index application

key_papers.title: Long-time methods for molecular dynamics simulations: Markov State Models and Milestoning
key_papers.journal: Progress in Molecular Biology and Translational Science
key_papers.relevance: MSM methods for protein aggregation — methodology paper

bridge_validity.basis: Both fields use identical Markov chain / Liouvillian eigenspectrum formalism. Amyloid MSMs (spectral gap governs aggregation rate) have exactly the mathematical structure analyzed by Klich et al. The Mpemba index is computable for any Markov chain.
bridge_validity.caveats: Amyloid MSMs typically describe aggregation, not thermalization — the 'temperature' in the Mpemba framework needs to map to a 'folding free energy' or denaturant concentration axis. This mapping is non-trivial but doable.

explored: Mpemba effect in spin systems, trapped ions, Brownian particles, granular gases
Amyloid kinetics via MSM and secondary nucleation (each field separately)
not_explored: Mpemba index of protein folding/misfolding Markov state models
Whether specific amyloidogenic sequences have non-normal Liouvillian dynamics in MSMs
Temperature-protocol optimization for amyloid suppression using eigenmode-overlap principles
Whether slower cooling = more misfolded (prion literature) has spectral explanation
gap_analysis.most_promising: Compute Mpemba index of known amyloid MSMs (Aβ42, α-synuclein, tau); predict which sequences/conditions yield exponentially faster fibril suppression under specific annealing protocols

candidates.field_c: Bacterial membrane biology — FMM-mediated AMP detection

key_papers.title: Studies on the mechanism of general anesthesia
key_papers.relevance: Definitive PLD2/GM1 raft relocalization mechanism paper

key_papers.title: In vivo characterization of the scaffold activity of flotillin on the membrane kinase KinC of Bacillus subtilis
key_papers.relevance: Bacterial FMM/FloT/KinC scaffold mechanism — prokaryotic counterpart

key_papers.title: Membrane Microdomain Disassembly Inhibits MRSA Antibiotic Resistance
key_papers.relevance: FMM role in antibiotic resistance — confirms FMM = AMP-relevant platform

key_papers.title: Flotillin-mediated stabilization of unfolded proteins in bacterial membrane microdomains
key_papers.relevance: Latest FMM function — protein quality control in bacterial FMMs (2024 breakthrough)

bridge_validity.basis: Both systems use lipid microdomain relocalization for signal transduction. Surfactin (cyclic lipopeptide, AMP-like) triggers KinC activation via FloT in bacterial FMMs — structurally analogous to anesthetic disruption of GM1 rafts triggering PLD2 relocalization.
bridge_validity.caveats: Bacterial PLD homologues (PldA) have different substrate specificity and localization than mammalian PLD2. The specific 'phospholipase → signaling lipid → effector' cascade in bacteria during AMP detection needs verification. FloT relocalization itself (not just KinC) upon AMP exposure is not documented.

explored: PLD2 relocalization cascade in eukaryotes (well characterized)
FMM organization and KinC scaffolding in bacteria (well characterized)
General raft-FMM structural analogy (acknowledged in both fields)
not_explored: Whether bacterial FMM disruption by AMPs activates phospholipase-like signaling
Whether FloT relocalization changes upon AMP exposure (not just KinC localization)
Specific signaling lipid production in bacteria during AMP detection
Whether surfactin-triggered KinC activation maps quantitatively onto PLD2 model
gap_analysis.most_promising: Identify whether AMP-triggered FMM disruption in bacteria produces PA-like signaling lipids via bacterial phospholipase, analogous to PLD2's PA production in anesthetic mechanism

candidates.field_a: Materials science — HEA thermodynamics, Hume-Rothery rules
bridge_concepts: mixing enthalpy as pairwise interaction matrix

key_papers.title: Exploring high entropy alloys: thermodynamic design and computational modeling
key_papers.relevance: HEA configurational entropy formula and Hume-Rothery rules — Field A anchor

key_papers.title: Beyond Hume-Rothery Rules
key_papers.relevance: Modern assessment: Hume-Rothery rules are necessary but insufficient — ML now outperforms them

key_papers.title: Key features and guidelines for the application of microbial alpha diversity metrics
key_papers.relevance: Shannon index in microbiome context — identical formula to HEA configurational entropy

key_papers.title: Optimal Microbiome Networks: Macroecology and Criticality
key_papers.relevance: Critical transitions and phase behavior in microbiome networks — Field C

bridge_validity.basis: Configurational entropy formula ΔS_conf = -RΣ xᵢ ln(xᵢ) is mathematically identical to Shannon diversity H = -Σ pᵢ log pᵢ. Mixing enthalpy (ΔH_mix) maps to pairwise cross-feeding/inhibition matrix. Hume-Rothery trait rules (size, structure, valence) map to ecological functional trait distance.
bridge_validity.caveats: Mathematical identity does not guarantee physical equivalence. Thermodynamic equilibrium in HEA vs. evolutionary/ecological dynamics in microbiome are different frameworks. The Hume-Rothery analogy is formal — no ecological data confirms trait-distance thresholds for coexistence.

explored: HEA entropy-enthalpy competition, Hume-Rothery rules, ML prediction (materials science)
Microbial Shannon diversity, species coexistence, pairwise interactions (ecology)
not_explored: Whether Hume-Rothery-like trait distance rules predict gut microbiome community composition
Whether mixing enthalpy matrix predicts single-community vs. partitioned ecology
Whether high-entropy microbiomes have phase transitions analogous to HEA phase separation
Applying HEA phase prediction algorithms to predict community collapse vs. stable states
gap_analysis.most_promising: Map gut microbiome pairwise interaction matrix onto alloy mixing enthalpy; test whether HEA phase prediction algorithms (ML or thermodynamic) predict community collapse vs. stable high-diversity states in human gut cohort data

candidates.field_c: Clinical oncology — tumor dormancy and recurrence timing

key_papers.title: Statistical Models for Earthquake Occurrences and Residual Analysis for Point Processes
key_papers.journal: Journal of the American Statistical Association
key_papers.relevance: Original ETAS model — the core formalism for the bridge

key_papers.title: Cancer recurrence times from a branching process model
key_papers.relevance: Cancer dormancy with branching process (NOT ETAS) — establishes what exists and what's missing

key_papers.title: Dormancy in Breast Cancer
key_papers.relevance: Clinical data on 20-year recurrence patterns — potential dataset for ETAS fitting

key_papers.title: A generalizable relationship between mortality and time-to-death among breast cancer patients explained by tumour dormancy
key_papers.relevance: Power-law-like temporal pattern in breast cancer mortality — potential Omori-law signature

bridge_validity.basis: Paracrine signaling provides physical mechanism for self-excitation: each reactivated tumor cell emits cytokines/growth factors that increase neighbor reactivation probability (aftershocks). ETAS branching ratio n maps onto paracrine amplification factor. Subcritical (n<1) = maintained dormancy; supercritical (n>1) = clinical recurrence. Omori-law decay = cytokine half-life decay after reactivation event.
bridge_validity.caveats: ETAS temporal clustering requires that each 'recurrence event' has a measurable aftershock sequence — clinical data typically captures only first recurrence, not the full cascade. Need cohort data with serial monitoring to test.

explored: Branching processes for cancer (independent events, not self-exciting)
Tumor dormancy mathematical models (immune, growth, stochastic)
not_explored: Self-exciting ETAS model where each micrometastasis reactivation triggers neighbors
Estimation of ETAS branching ratio n from breast cancer recurrence timing data
Omori-law analysis of recurrence risk decay after adjuvant therapy
Subcritical/supercritical phase transition as function of immune pressure/treatment
gap_analysis.most_promising: Fit ETAS model to breast cancer recurrence time-series across large cohort (e.g., METABRIC, TCGA); test whether n < 1 characterizes dormant patients and n → 1 predicts breakthrough recurrence better than current clinical models

candidates.field_a: Statistical physics — stochastic resetting theory (Evans & Majumdar 2011)
candidates.field_c: Infectious disease microbiology — antibiotic persister clearance
candidates.adjacent_work_note: Singh et al. 2023 derive optimal pulse dosing deterministically; stochastic clearance first-passage papers exist — but neither uses Evans-Majumdar resetting framework

key_papers.title: Diffusion with Stochastic Resetting
key_papers.relevance: Foundational resetting theory with optimal r* — the core formalism for the bridge

key_papers.title: Systematic design of pulse dosing to eradicate persister bacteria
key_papers.relevance: Pulse dosing for persisters — closest existing work; deterministic, NOT resetting framework

key_papers.title: Theoretical investigation of stochastic clearance of bacteria: first-passage analysis
key_papers.journal: Journal of The Royal Society Interface
key_papers.relevance: First-passage analysis of bacterial clearance — adjacent math but no dosing-as-resetting

key_papers.title: Antibiotic-induced population fluctuations and stochastic clearance of bacteria
key_papers.relevance: Stochastic eradication dynamics — establishes the stochastic nature of the problem

bridge_validity.basis: Mapping: particle position → persister population fraction; resetting event → antibiotic dose administration; target → population extinction; optimal r* → optimal dosing interval. Renewal equation from Evans-Majumdar directly translates to analytic formula for optimal dosing interval. Singh et al. 2023 find optimal ratio numerically — resetting theory provides closed-form derivation from first principles.
bridge_validity.caveats: Antibiotic dosing is not a clean instantaneous reset — pharmacokinetics create a concentration time-course rather than an ideal reset. Need to map realistic PK curves onto 'reset quality' parameter in extended resetting theory.

explored: Stochastic resetting for diffusive search, molecular dynamics sampling
Antibiotic persister pulse dosing (deterministic ODE approach)
Stochastic clearance first-passage analysis (adjacent framework, no resetting)
not_explored: Mapping persister pharmacodynamics onto Evans-Majumdar resetting framework
Analytical derivation of optimal dosing interval from stochastic resetting r*
Whether persister resuscitation stochasticity creates an optimal resetting-rate analogue
Multi-drug dosing as multi-particle resetting to different targets
gap_analysis.most_promising: Derive optimal antibiotic dosing interval using renewal equation from Evans & Majumdar; map persister-to-sensitive switching rate onto first-passage time to extinction; test prediction against Singh 2023 numerical results and existing clinical dosing data

candidates.field_c: Orthopedic biomechanics — OA cartilage tipping point
candidates.shared_math_framework: Poroelastic theory (Terzaghi/Biot consolidation) underlies both fields — but no one imports the liquefaction-specific CSR/N_liq/r_u diagnostic framework into biomechanics

key_papers.title: Evaluating the Potential for Liquefaction or Cyclic Failure of Silts and Clays
key_papers.relevance: CSR/CRR/N_liq framework for soil liquefaction — the formalism to be imported

key_papers.title: Biphasic creep and stress relaxation of articular cartilage in compression
key_papers.relevance: Biphasic cartilage theory — the mathematical counterpart in biomechanics

key_papers.title: Osteoarthritis year in review 2023: Biomechanics
key_papers.relevance: Current state of OA biomechanics — establishes lack of cycle-counting failure criterion

key_papers.title: Exploring mechanisms governing cartilage interstitial fluid load support in lubrication
key_papers.relevance: Interstitial fluid pressure dynamics in cartilage — direct analog of pore pressure in soils

bridge_validity.basis: Both are poroelastic systems with cyclic loading → pore pressure buildup → shear/structural strength loss. CSR maps to cartilage loading intensity / strength ratio. r_u maps to cartilage fluid pressure ratio. N_liq maps to cycles to OA tipping point. Drained-undrained transition (rate-dependent in soil) maps to cartilage frequency-dependent response (high frequency = undrained, low frequency = drained).
bridge_validity.caveats: Soil liquefaction is an irreversible catastrophic failure in seconds; OA is a progressive process over years. The timescale mismatch may require adapting N_liq to cumulative damage model rather than single-event failure. Also: cartilage is viscoelastic (not purely poroelastic) and has biological repair capacity that soil lacks.

explored: Cyclic loading effects on OA (qualitative, 2022-2024 reviews)
not_explored: Cyclic stress ratio equivalent for articular cartilage under physiological loading
N_liq-like critical cycle counting for cumulative OA cartilage damage
Pore pressure ratio r_u as OA progression biomarker
Whether OA tipping point satisfies FS < 1 criterion analogue
Whether cartilage fatigue follows N ∝ σ^{-b} power law from soil geotechnics
gap_analysis.most_promising: Develop a Cartilage Liquefaction Index (CLI) analogous to CSR-based liquefaction potential; test whether cumulative loading above threshold N_OA predicts irreversible OA progression in longitudinal cohort data (MOST trial, UK Biobank gait data)

summary.generator_strategy_recommendation: For DISJOINT candidates (1,3,4,5,6): use free parametric generation — no existing cross-field papers to avoid. For PARTIALLY_EXPLORED candidate (2): identify the specific mechanistic gap (AMP-triggered phospholipase signaling in bacteria) and generate around that gap. All 6 bridges are mathematically valid.
summary.disjointness_hard_constraint_note: 5/6 candidates are DISJOINT with confidence >= 0.90. Per pipeline rules, orchestrator should prefer DISJOINT candidates. Candidate 2 (PARTIALLY_EXPLORED) should only be selected if the mechanistic gap is clearly identified in the hypothesis.
